What is Storage?

Every title on Xbox Live has a data store associated with it. Your title can use this storage to distribute data to subscribers. It can also use its storage to enable players to exchange data. How to use the global data storage area is up to you as a game designer. For example, you could use storage to do the following.

  • Distribute new level maps to all players.
  • Give new weapons and power-ups to players.
  • Enable players to exchange custom maps that they create with your title on their individual Xbox consoles. This scenario presupposes your title includes a level editor.

Note    Storage is not intended to be used as a means of updating executable files distributed with your game. For that task, Xbox Live provides the Autoupdate feature.

Content downloaded through the Xbox Live storage service is stored and managed in the T:\$K directory. This content cannot be treated as a Save Game and cannot be copied between Xbox consoles. However, the dashboard enables players to delete all content stored in the T:\$K directory.

  • Types of Storage
  • Storage and Family Title IDs
  • Using Storage Content Offline

Types of Storage

Storage can be allocated for the following purposes.

Global storage
Global storage should generally only be used for data that is to be distributed to all players. The maximum space available for global storage is 5 MB and 50 files.
Per User
Every title can have a small amount of space allocated for each user. The maximum space available for per user storage is 64 KB or 8 files.
Per Team
Each team in a given title can have a dedicated storage area reserved for team-specific data. The maximum space available for per team storage is 64 KB in one file plus 8 KB in one file per team member. Because teams can have a maximum of 100 members, the maximum available storage is 864 KB. The maximum number of available files is 101.
Per Message
Titles can use storage to attach custom, game-specific binary data to messages. The maximum space available is 64 KB of file attachments per message.
Per Leaderboard (Game Clips)
Titles can use storage for attachments to statistics. For example, ghost races of the top 10 races on a leaderboard would be stored as game clips. The maximum space available for game clips is 1 GB per title and 2 MB per game clip.

Storage and Family Title IDs

In addition, storage can be used across families of titles. That is, you can post data to storage that can be used by more than one of your titles. For instance, suppose you publish a game that lets players build cities that others can come and visit. Imagine further that you also publish a game that enables players to fly a helicopter around cities. You have designed both games such that both games can use the same city map. In this case, you can post new city maps to storage and that players in both games can download. For more information, see XOnlineStorageSetFamilyTitleID.

Using Storage Content Offline

If you want to use content obtained from Xbox Live storage in an offline capacity, you will need to sign the content a second time with an offline signature at the time the content is written to the Xbox console hard disk. Your title should always check the content for Xbox Live whenever the user is signed on. If the player is using the content offline, it can instead check only the offline signature. Refer to the white paper on Xbox Content Signing Options for more information.

If you want to enable offline sharing of Xbox Live content, you need to migrate the content to the U:\ drive using the save functions. This content directory should still carry both the offline and the online signatures. The presence of the signatures enables the player to use the dashboard management facilities and MU roamability of the individual pieces of content.
